title: Protocol for Lambda Exonuclease (NEB #M0262)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9bcv4","text":"Lambda Exonuclease efficiently degrades 5' phosphorylated linear dsDNA from 5' to 3' direction, leaving supercoiled dsDNA intact.*","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"66rin","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1soct","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"22kvi","text":"*Note: For more precise results we recommend titration of the enzyme to the intended substrate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"dgucg","text":"Please see SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for hazards and safety warnings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Stop reaction by adding EDTA to 10 millimolar (mM) .

To clean up treated samples, we recommend using one of the following steps : a. Column clean up (we recommend the Monarch® PCR & DNA Cleanup Kit, NEB #T1030 ) or b. Running the reaction on an agarose gel, and then extracting the DNA (we recommend the Monarch Gel Extraction Kit, NEB #T1020 ), or c. Performing a phenol/chloroform extraction followed by ethanol precipitation.
creator: New England Biolabs Tech Support
